differentials.name: Secondary brainstem injury
differentials.raw: Secondary brainstem injury (mechanical compression, secondary (Duret) hemorrhage in tegmentum of rostral pons + midbrain, infarction of median / paramedian perforating arteries, necrosis)

differentials.name: Duret hemorrhage =  delayed 2ndary hemorrhage in ventral + paramedian aspects of upper brainstem (mesencephalon + pons) due to massive temporal lobe herniation causing stretching + laceration of pontine perforating branches of basilar artery
differentials.raw: Duret hemorrhage =  delayed 2ndary hemorrhage in ventral + paramedian aspects of upper brainstem (mesencephalon + pons) due to massive temporal lobe herniation causing stretching + laceration of pontine perforating branches of basilar artery

differentials.name: Kernohan phenomenon =  contusion of contralateral brainstem caused by pressure of free edge of tentorium [ipsilateral pupillary dilatation]
differentials.raw: Kernohan phenomenon =  contusion of contralateral brainstem caused by pressure of free edge of tentorium

differentials.name: Direct impact on brain ← fracture / skull distortion
differentials.raw: Direct impact on brain ← fracture / skull distortion

differentials.name: Indirect injury irrespective of skull deformation
differentials.raw: Indirect injury irrespective of skull deformation

differentials.name: Brainstem + deep gray matter nuclei ›  linear acceleration forces
differentials.raw: Brainstem + deep gray matter nuclei ›  linear acceleration forces (less common)

context: Location:   cingulate, uncal, parahippocampal gyri, cerebellar tonsils Pathophysiology: expanding supratentorial mass forces medial aspect of temporal lobe downward over tentorium compressing the neighboring oculomotor nerve (III); lateral pressure on midbrain compresses opposite crus cerebri against free edge of tentorium forming indentation in crus (Kernohan notch) Pathomechanism: √  scalp / skull abnormal √  superficial neural damage localized to immediate vicinity of calvarial injury √  scalp / skull normal (a)  compression-rarefaction strain = change in cell volume without change in shape
